wrong_conclusion: Total revenue is stable, system is healthy
why_wrong: Ignores per-trip decline; total = volume × per-unit
right_conclusion: Revenue per trip is declining — drivers are poorer
correction_method: Decompose total into volume × per-unit metrics

wrong_conclusion: More trips = more profit for drivers
why_wrong: Ignores revenue per trip; more work ≠ more pay
right_conclusion: Drivers work harder for same money; system is inefficient
correction_method: Calculate revenue per trip and driver hourly wage

why_wrong: Doesn't control for payment type; cash tips underreported
right_conclusion: Credit card tips are similar; cash data is incomplete
correction_method: Filter to credit card payments only for tip analysis

wrong_conclusion: Any trip with a timestamp is valid
why_wrong: Includes impossible data (zero distance, negative fare)
right_conclusion: Real trips need valid distance, time, and locations
correction_method: Apply data quality filters with documented rationale

why_wrong: Extreme fares skew average; median is better
right_conclusion: Median fare better represents typical trip
correction_method: Use median instead of mean; remove outliers
